title: Unified Musical Object (UMO)
description: Canonical per-track artifact schema for Helix. Defined by the HIL Protocol (core/hil/PROTOCOL.md). Principle: same invariant, different observability. All dialect views are optional except where required by partial observability rules; top-level structural fields are required.

entity_id.description: Canonical Helix entity ID for this track. Format: music.track:<slug>. Slug is lowercase alphanumeric with underscores and hyphens.

representations.description: The four observability layers of the UMO. Each is an independent partial view of the same underlying musical object. No layer is ground truth.

causal.description: Chip/VGM register-level representation. Produced by Nuked adapters. Models time evolution of operator topology, routing, envelopes, and channel state. May be absent if no causal source is available. Extensible: additional dialect-specific fields are permitted.

operator_topology.description: Per-channel operator routing derived from algorithm register. Maps channel ID to operator roles (modulator/carrier) and connection graph.

carrier_slots.description: Operator slot indices that output to DAC per algorithm. Derived from Nuked adapter carrier_slots tables.

modulator_behavior.description: Per-modulator parameters: MULT, DT1, TL, AR, DR, D1L, D2R, RR as time sequences.

feedback_profile.description: Feedback register values per channel over time. High feedback = nonlinear self-excitation = spectral brightness.

envelope_shapes.description: ADSR envelope parameter sequences per operator per channel. Encodes temporal shaping decisions.

channel_usage.description: Active channel timeline: which channels are keyed on/off, at what times, with what patches.

temporal_trajectories.description: Ordered sequence of register-change events. Each entry is a timestamped state delta, not a static snapshot.

symbolic.description: Symbolic/musicological representation. Derived from MIDI or audio pitch tracking. Lossy projection — treat as hypothesis, not truth. May be absent if no MIDI or pitch-trackable source is available. Extensible: additional dialect-specific fields are permitted.

melody.description: Melodic content: pitch sequences, intervals, contour. Derived from dominant voice or pitch tracking.

harmony.description: Harmonic content: chord sequences, key estimation, harmonic rhythm.

rhythm.description: Rhythmic content: tempo, meter, onset patterns, rhythmic density.

voice_structure.description: Polyphonic voice layout: number of voices, voice-leading relationships, parallel motion.

perceptual.description: Perceptual/audio feature representation. Extracted from rendered waveform or audio source. Minimum baseline — always computable when audio is available. Extensible: additional dialect-specific fields are permitted.

timbre_descriptors.description: MFCC vectors, spectral shape descriptors, zero-crossing rate, and other timbral summaries.

spectral_profile.description: Spectral centroid, bandwidth, rolloff, and flux over time.

brightness.description: Normalized spectral brightness: 0 = dark/low-frequency dominant, 1 = bright/high-frequency dominant.

roughness.description: Perceived roughness / dissonance estimate. Related to beating between partials.

attack_character.description: Attack transient features: rise time, peak sharpness, transient density per unit time.

polyphony_estimate.description: Estimated number of simultaneous voices. Real-valued to allow fractional confidence.

metadata.description: Declared metadata layer. Not ground truth. recorded = raw tag values; normalized = normalized/coerced values for indexing. Never merged with inferred structure.

recorded.description: Raw values parsed from .vgz.tag or equivalent foobar2000-dialect tag file. All fields optional. Missing fields represent absence, not failure. Incorrect values must be preserved exactly as declared.

date.description: Raw date string from tag. Not parsed to date type.

featuring.description: Featured artist(s). String when single value from tag; array when multiple. Normalization to array occurs in metadata.normalized.

sound_team.description: Sound team member(s). String when single value from tag; array when multiple. Normalization to array occurs in metadata.normalized.

platform.description: Hardware platform (e.g. Mega Drive, SNES, PS1).

sound_chip.description: Sound chip(s) declared in tag. String when single value from tag; array when multiple. Informs analysis expectations but does not replace analysis.

normalized.description: Normalized/indexed form of recorded metadata. May include slug forms, resolved entity IDs, or coerced types. May be empty.

alignment_map.description: Explicit cross-layer alignment entries. Required (may be empty). All invariants must pass through alignment_map. Entries must respect temporal consistency — do not align frame-level causal events with full-track symbolic summaries.

causal.description: Causal-layer feature or pattern being aligned. Optional — omit when causal data is unavailable; do not fabricate.

perceptual.description: Perceptual-layer feature being aligned. Optional — omit when perceptual data is unavailable; do not fabricate.

symbolic.description: Symbolic-layer feature being aligned. Optional — omit when symbolic data is unavailable; do not fabricate.

temporal_scope.description: Time range this alignment covers. E.g. '0.0-4.2s', 'verse_1', 'full_track'.

granularity.description: Temporal granularity of this alignment entry.

causal_confidence.description: Confidence in the causal observation underlying this alignment.

perceptual_confidence.description: Confidence in the perceptual observation underlying this alignment.

symbolic_confidence.description: Confidence in the symbolic observation underlying this alignment.

conflicts.description: Cross-layer disagreements. Required (may be empty). Conflicts are first-class data — never discard. May indicate transcription error, perceptual ambiguity, or structural tension. DO NOT RESOLVE automatically.

confidence.description: Confidence that a genuine conflict exists (vs. measurement noise).

invariants.description: Patterns that survive across representations. Required (may be empty). STRICT: reject format-specific artifacts and single-layer patterns. NOTE: the ≥2 dialect evidence requirement is a protocol rule enforced during extraction, not a structural schema constraint — a single-dialect evidence object must still be rejected by the extracting agent per STEP 6.

evidence.description: Evidence per dialect. Protocol requires ≥2 dialects to be populated for an invariant to be valid. Schema allows partial population but extraction agent must enforce the ≥2 rule.

evidence_dialects.description: Dialects that contributed evidence for this invariant. Must contain ≥2 entries — complements the evidence object and makes the multi-dialect requirement structurally enforceable.

confidence.description: Confidence this pattern is a representation-invariant structure and not a format artifact.

identity.description: Composer identity model inferred from this track. Identity is a stable pattern of decisions across representations — not format, chip, or instrument.

inferred_profile.description: Representation-invariant structural summary of composer/artist identity. Encodes stable decision patterns across all available dialects — topology preferences, feedback tendencies, envelope behavior, channel allocation strategy, temporal modulation patterns. Not tied to any single format or chip.

evidence_tracks.description: List of entity_ids (music.track:<slug> format) used to derive this identity profile. Empty array for single-track analysis; populated when identity is inferred across multiple works.
